title: L1 playbook docs/playbooks
purpose: D1.2 defines l1 playbook docs playbooks for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D1.2` only; siblings under `D1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: Every conductor and worker session loads always-on context—[E4.1](E4.1-context-always-on-rules-agents-md.md) rules and `AGENTS.md`—as layer zero before hooks ([E4.2](E4.2-context-hooks-inject-continue-start.md)) or Librarian `allowed_reads` ([E4.3](E4.3-context-allowed-reads-cap-max-5.md)) narrow the turn.
`AGENTS.md` encodes the baseline pursuit contract: evidence gates, H1/H2/H3 approval semantics, journal dual-write, and pipeline phase order that the genius conductor enforces each wake.
Economy workers inherit applicable rule excerpts in spawn context; they do not re-read the full `AGENTS.md` monolith unless the conductor bound it in `allowed_reads`.
Active template packs may extend always-on rules via pack fragments merged at role switch so company-specific policy layers atop repo defaults without replacing core conductor obligations.

When `AGENTS.md` or always-on rules change, [E5.2](E5.2-staleness-extend-playbooks-scripts-pack-nodes.md) marks dependent playbooks and skills stale and pursuit pauses at H2 until reconcile-stale or operator H1 confirms downstream artifacts are current.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D1.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D1.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D1.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D1.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D1.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D1.2 defines l1 playbook docs playbooks for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system every conductor and worker session loads always-on context—[e4.1](e4.1-context-always-on-rules-agents-md.md) rules and `agents.md`—as layer zero before hooks ([e4.2](e4.2-context-hooks-inject-continue-start.md)) or librarian `allowed_reads` ([e4.3](e4.3-context-allowed-reads-cap-max-5.md)) narrow the turn, then `agents.md` encodes the baseline pursuit contract: evidence gates, h1/h2/h3 approval semantics, journal dual-write, and pipeline phase order that the genius conductor enforces each wake.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

L1 promotes distilled procedure into `docs/playbooks/<slug>.md`: step lists, constraints, and pointers that humans and agents can follow without re-reading full design monoliths. Playbook-keeper skill ([D4.1](D4.1-platform-work-playbook-keeper.md)) is the primary platform worker for L1 creation from divergence logs and task post-mortems.

An L1 playbook is not a script—it encodes intent and ordering while leaving file-derived steps to future L2 extraction. INDEX rows and task-card references ([D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md), [D6.3](D6.3-platform-done-task-card-references.md)) prove the playbook is discoverable. Stale upstream design should bump dependents via Plane E staleness, not silent drift.
